channels.name: meridian-chen
channels.topic: Meridian Biosciences v. Chen Labs trial prep
channels.purpose: Case coordination for Meridian patent litigation. Trial April 14.
channels.creator: U_AORTIZ

messages.text: Running 5 min late to standup. Start without me.
Q4 all-hands next Wednesday Oct 22 at 2 PM in the main conference room. We'll cover firm financials, new client wins, and year-end planning. Calendar invite going out now.
Got it. Thanks David.
Thank you for the reminder. I will be there.
Good standup. Fatima, send me the TheraCure claim chart by EOD.
Understood. I will have it to you by 5.
Good meeting today. Slides are in the shared drive. Lisa will send benefits enrollment info next week -- don't miss the deadline.
Benefits open enrollment is live. Check your email for the portal link and plan details. Deadline is November 30. Please don't wait until the last minute.
Thanks Lisa.
Shifting focus to Meridian discovery. Grayson take lead on the privilege log. Fatima, continue the prior art mapping.
On it. How many docs are we looking at?
Around 400. Lisa's team pulled the initial batch.
WFH today, plumber coming for a kitchen issue. Available on Slack if anyone needs me.
No worries. Ping me if you need anything on the Meridian front.
I hope everything goes smoothly! I have a quick question about the TheraCure portfolio review when you have a moment.
Sure Fatima, send it over.

Hey -- TheraCure just escalated. Neil Kapoor called me. NovaBion filed a continuation on the T-47 compound class and he says it overlaps with claims 4-8 of the '482. Wants to talk strategy -- can you do 10:45?
How broad is the continuation?
Haven't seen the full filing but Kapoor's in-house team thinks claims 1-3 map directly onto the same structural class. Fatima pulled the prosecution history last week so she's up to speed.
Ok. 10:45 works. Need to move a few things.
Perfect. I'll send the Zoom link. Looping Fatima in too.
Sounds good.
Zoom link sent to your email. Kapoor confirmed 10:45.
Got it.
Hi Adriana, Grayson mentioned the TheraCure call at 10:45. I have the '482 prosecution history and the claim chart from last month's review. Should I focus on anything specific?
Claims 4 through 8. Have the file wrapper open.
Understood. I'll be ready.
Good call. Kapoor seemed relieved once you walked through the prior art landscape.
Agreed. Have Fatima draft the FTO by Friday.
Already talked to her. She says she can have a first pass by Thursday.
Even better. I want to review before it goes out.
Understood.
That was a very productive call. I'm drafting the summary and action items now. Should I send directly to you, or copy Grayson as well?
Send to me first. I'll review.
Of course. I'll have it to you within the hour.
Anyone up for happy hour Friday? New place opened on M Street.
Can't this week, busy weekend. Next time though.
I would be happy to join! What time were you thinking?
6:30? I'll send the address.
Good morning! How was everyone's Thanksgiving?
Great, cooked a lot. How about you?
Wonderful, thank you for asking! My family came down from Philadelphia. We made way too much food.
Ate too much. Back to reality.
Standup moved to 10:30 today. Conflict with a client call.
Works for me.
Reminder -- holiday party this Thursday at Iron Gate! 6 PM. 22 people confirmed so far. Plus-ones still welcome, just let me know by end of day.
Can't wait. Same spot as last year or somewhere new?
Same spot! They loved having us last year and gave us the whole patio room again.
Looking forward to it. Are plus-ones still open? I have a friend visiting that week.
Of course! Just send me their name so I can update the headcount.
See everyone there.
Any dress code situation or just business casual?
Holiday festive is encouraged but not required. Just no jeans please.
Looking forward to it. Thanks for organizing this, Lisa.
Last night was so much fun! The food at Iron Gate was incredible.
Agreed. That lamb was excellent. And Fatima -- the baklava was a hit.
So happy everyone had a good time! Already talking to Iron Gate about next year.
Adriana, any fun plans this weekend?
Nothing much. Errands and catching up on sleep hopefully. You?
Holiday shopping mostly. Have a great weekend everyone!
Same here. Good weekend all.
Standup at 10. Have your updates ready.
Happy new year everyone. Q1 all-hands Wednesday Jan 7 at 2 PM. Lot to cover -- Meridian trial timeline, new associate onboarding, and 2026 goals. See you there.
See you there.
Thanks everyone for a productive session. Action items posted in the shared drive. Welcome aboard to our new associates.
Revisiting the Claim 7 dependency chain -- can we discuss tomorrow?
Added trial prep blocks to the calendar through April. Weekly claims review starting Monday. Grayson, I need you to own the damages expert coordination. Fatima, claims construction research.
Sure -- I'm free after standup. The '512 continuation might be the weak link if Chen argues prosecution history estoppel. Want me to pull the file wrapper before we talk?
Action items from standup: Grayson -- damages expert shortlist by Friday. Fatima -- finalize Claim 3 dependency chart. I'll draft the trial timeline.
Dr. Reeves is my top pick for damages. Want me to reach out?
Do it.
Got your reply to Johnston. Good call pushing back on the January 24 deadline. One thing -- should we file a motion to compel on the privilege log or keep it in the meet and confer?
Meet and confer first. Only escalate if they stonewall.
Pull the prior art from the Chen prosecution history. Full chain including continuations.
Good morning, Adriana. I will pull the full prosecution history for the Chen '847 patent today. Should I also include the PCT filing and any related continuation chain?
Yes. All of it.
Hey Adriana -- quick question on the damages model. Should we use the Georgia-Pacific factors or go with the analytical approach? I'm leaning GP but wanted your take before the expert meeting.
GP. Don't overthink it.
Hi Adriana, would you like me to have the prior art memo ready by Friday or is early next week acceptable?
Friday.
Fatima -- I owe you an apology. I was short with you in the review yesterday and that wasn't fair. Your research was solid. I should have let you finish.
Thank you, Adriana. I appreciate you saying that. I understand things are busy right now.
It won't happen again. Keep doing what you're doing.
Adriana, checking in on the self-assessment. Jan 31 deadline. Any questions?
On track. You'll have it by the 30th.
Self-assessment submitted. Check your inbox.
Got it. I'll review this weekend. Thanks Adriana.
IT is pushing the quarterly password reset today. Check your email for the link. If you run into issues email helpdesk@kendricksloane.com.

Adriana, I have completed the initial dependency mapping for Claims 3 and 7. The '512 continuation introduces a narrowing amendment that may affect Claim 7's scope. I have a draft chart ready for your review.
Good start. Focus on the prosecution history for the '512 -- that's where Chen's estoppel argument will land. Bring the chart to Monday's session.
Read through your self-assessment. Strong year. Let's discuss at our next 1:1.
Sounds good. Thanks David.
Expert deposition week starts Monday. Conference Room A all week. Grayson -- direct examination on Dr. Reeves. Fatima -- have the claim charts and prosecution history binders ready for each session.
All prepped. Reeves is solid -- ran through his testimony framework yesterday.
Binders are organized and indexed. I will have them in the conference room by 8 AM Monday.
Quick question on the damages model -- GP or analytical for the expert meeting? Leaning GP but wanted your call.
GP. Royalty base is clean and the comparable licenses are strong. Analytical opens us up on apportionment. Lock it in with Reeves.
Got it. Sending the license comparables to Reeves today.
Understood, I will integrate the GP framework into the claims narrative as well.
Day 3 done. Reeves was strong on the GP factors. Chen's counsel struggled on cross with the comparable licenses.
Good. Tomorrow's the damages expert meeting with Reeves at 2. Bring the comparable licenses binder.

Adriana, the Claim 3/7 dependency analysis is complete. I have mapped the full prosecution history including the '512 continuation and identified three potential estoppel arguments Chen may raise. The memo is in your inbox.
Reviewed. Solid work. The estoppel mapping on Claim 7 is exactly what we needed. Let's discuss the rebuttal strategy Monday.
Adriana, I have submitted the Markman brief draft to the shared drive. I believe the claim construction analysis is thorough but I would value your feedback on the estoppel section in particular.
I'll review tonight. First impression from skimming -- this looks really solid Fatima.
Fatima's draft of the Markman brief is strong. Clean claim construction, well-sourced prosecution history. Team should review by Monday -- this is close to final.
Thank you, Adriana. I will incorporate any feedback from the team and have the revised version ready by end of day Monday.
Hey Adriana -- heard your car's in the shop. Want a ride to the office tomorrow?
That would be great, thanks. I can be ready by 8.
I'll swing by 8:15. Brookland right?
Yes. 3847 12th St NE. Thanks Grayson.
Outside. Take your time.
Coming down.
Last day of chauffeur service? Same time?
Actually picking it up this afternoon. But seriously, thank you. I owe you lunch.
Ha -- I'll take you up on that. Glad it's done.
Trial prep reminder -- Meridian v. Chen trial begins April 14. Adriana's team has priority on Conference Room A through trial. Everyone else please use B or the small rooms.
Thanks David. We'll try to share the room when we can.
Six weeks to trial. Weekly trial prep meetings start Monday. Same time as standup -- 10 AM but block 90 min.
Calendar updated. Should we invite anyone from the business side?
Not yet. Just us three until we're closer.
Spring office hours reminder: we're still 9 AM to 6 PM through April. Summer flex schedule starts May 1.
Looking forward to summer hours already.
I'm taking Friday off. Camping trip. You're good to cover standup?
Absolutely. Enjoy the outdoors. Where are you headed?
Shenandoah. Old Rag if the weather holds.
Nice. Old Rag is a beast. Have fun.
